metadata.title:
RAISE FUNDS
FOR STRIKERS

SYMPATHY AND FINANCIAL
AID GIVEN THE PHIL


ADELPHIA CAR MEN

pages.raw_text:
An audience of 900 people gather
ed asst night to extend sympathy
and financial aid to the striking
car men at Philadelphia and the
editorial staff of Solidarity, impris7
oned at New Castle, Pa, by officials
controlled by the steel trust.


The clubbing of the car men by
the police of Philadelphia was lik-
ened to the treatment extended by
the Spokane police department to
the l. w. WIS in the recent free
speech fight. When the police de-
partment of Spokane was charged
with the murder of S. O. Chinn,
who was buried this afternoon,
there were cheers that reached to
police headquarters, close at hand.


James p. Thompson and Fred w.
HeslewoOd were the chief speakers,
Thompson spoke of the Philadel-
phia strike, the brutalities that were
inflicted and the murders that were
committed in the name of the law
He said that the paper Solidarity
has been instrumental in uniting
the steel workers in the district
adjacent to Newcastle, Pa, and for
this, on trumped charge. the edi-
tors were thrown into Jail, at the
inStigation of the steel trust.


Heslewood gave more attention
to local affairs. He said the |. W.
w. members had suffered every
thing, even unto death, in the re-
cent speech fight, he said that the
workers had gained victory. He
cited numerous instances in which
the laws are being violated in Spo-
kane daily by favored ones at the
city hall, and insisted that if an
|. w. w. were to rise on street
corner to repeat the Declaration of
independence he would be thrown
into the sweat box and put on
bread and water. A collection of
about $40 was taken for the relief
fund.
